CheckTitle: Ensure weak MFA methods (SMS, voice) are disabled
Description: Checks whether SMS or voice call authentication methods are enabled in the tenant authentication methods policy. These methods are vulnerable to SIM-swapping and SS7 interception attacks.
Risk: SMS and voice calls are the weakest MFA factors. SIM-swap attacks allow adversaries to intercept one-time codes. NIST SP 800-63B deprecated SMS as an out-of-band authenticator. Attackers routinely target SMS-based MFA in credential phishing campaigns.

Remediation.Recommendation: Disable SMS and voice call authentication methods in the Entra ID authentication methods policy. Migrate users to Microsoft Authenticator, FIDO2 security keys, or Windows Hello for Business.

Frameworks.source: AI-generated mapping — not yet verified against published benchmark
AI-generated mapping — not yet verified against published benchmark

SourceNotes: NIST SP 800-63B explicitly discourages SMS-based authentication. CIS control ID is AI-generated.
